marketState: neutral — exceptional summer heat demand offsets bearish storage surplus and weak 4-week seasonality
summary: Front month (Aug-26) at $3.332 sits slightly above the current fair-value midpoint of $2.960 but within the $2.460–$3.460 band, indicating a weather premium is priced in but price is not stretched to extremes. The dominant near-term force is extraordinary cooling demand: both the 1-7D (+29.7% vs 5y average, above 5y maximum) and the 8-16D windows (+56.1% vs 5y average, above 5y maximum) are running at multi-year extremes simultaneously — a genuinely rare setup. Against this, seasonal probabilities are firmly negative over four weeks (only 19% of years show a higher price, typical move -1.8%) and storage sits +151 Bcf above the 5y average after a +6 Bcf bearish injection surprise, representing a structural supply overhang. Managed-money net positioning is at the 17th 5-year percentile (historically very net short at -84,909), which is a contrarian bullish signal that could amplify any weather-driven squeeze but may unwind quickly once heat fades. The daily and weekly charts retain a bearish 50/200 EMA cross, while intraday frames (5m–4h) are bullish with RSI overbought on the shortest frames, suggesting the current move is near-term extended.

longTerm.rationale: Seasonal probability is firmly negative — only 19% of years post a gain over four weeks, the typical four-week move is -1.8%, and the highest-reliability time to short NG is late June through August when injection-season storage builds weigh on price.
